title: Symplectic symmetry of quadratic-band-touching Hamiltonians in two dimensions
authors: Igor F. Herbut, Samson C. H. Ling
abstract: The internal low-energy symmetry of the massless Lorentz-invariant Dirac Hamiltonian in $2+1$ dimensions is known to be $O(2N)$, where $N$ is the number of two-component Dirac fermions. Here we point out that there exists an analogous internal symmetry of the single-particle quadratic-band-touching Hamiltonian in two spatial dimensions, and it is the unitary symplectic group, $USp(2N)$. All fermionic bilinears belong to one of the three small irreducible representations of this group. The interacting theory that respects the $USp(2N)$ symmetry and the spatial rotations is constructed and found to allow two independent interaction terms. When these interactions are infrared-relevant the symplectic symmetry either remains preserved or becomes spontaneously broken to $USp(N) \times USp(N)$. The symmetry in the lattices such as honeycomb to infinite order in the dispersion's expansion in powers of local momentum is given by the overlap of the symplectic and the orthogonal groups. We show that this overlap is $O(2N) \bigcap USp(2N) = U(N)$.
summary: This paper identifies a new internal symmetry, the unitary symplectic group USp(2N), for 2D Hamiltonians with quadratic-band-touching. It provides a complete classification of fermion bilinears under this symmetry and explores how interactions can lead to spontaneous symmetry breaking. Furthermore, it demonstrates that the symmetry of systems with both even and odd momentum-reversal terms is the intersection group U(N). This work is significant as it marks the first instance of a symplectic group emerging as the fundamental symmetry of a quantum Hamiltonian in this context.
main_problem: The paper aims to identify and classify the internal low-energy symmetry group of two-dimensional quadratic-band-touching (QBT) Hamiltonians and analyze their stability under interactions.
main_result: The authors discover that QBT Hamiltonians possess a unitary symplectic symmetry, USp(2N), and show that the symmetry of systems containing both even and odd momentum-reversal terms is the intersection group U(N).
method: The study employs group theory, Majorana fermion representations, and Renormalization Group (RG) analysis to classify fermion bilinears and investigate symmetry-breaking patterns.
model_or_system: The model focuses on 2D semimetals with quadratic-band-touching, such as Bernal-stacked bilayer graphene and hopping models on checkerboard or Kagome lattices, using a single-particle Hamiltonian for N two-component complex fermions.
key_observables: Fermion bilinears (masses and nematics), symmetry group dimensions, and Renormalization Group flow of interaction couplings.
important_parameters: Number of fermion flavors (N), interaction coupling constants (g1, g2, g3), and momentum-reversal parity (even vs. odd).
main_assumptions: The analysis initially assumes a single-particle Hamiltonian structure and focuses on the low-energy limit where the dispersion can be expanded in powers of local momentum.
paper_structure: The paper first reviews the O(2N) symmetry of Dirac Hamiltonians, then derives the USp(2N) symmetry for even-parity QBT Hamiltonians, classifies fermion bilinears, constructs the interacting theory, and finally analyzes the symmetry overlap in lattices like the honeycomb lattice.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
